original_query: with customer_total_return as  (select wr_returning_customer_sk as ctr_customer_sk  ,ca_state as ctr_state ,wr_reason_sk as ctr_reason_sk ,sum(wr_return_amt) as ctr_total_return  from web_returns      ,date_dim      ,customer_address      ,item  where wr_returned_date_sk = d_date_sk    and d_year =2001    and wr_returning_addr_sk = ca_address_sk    and wr_item_sk = i_item_sk    and i_manager_id BETWEEN 69 and 78    and wr_return_amt / wr_return_quantity between 28 and 57  group by wr_returning_customer_sk          ,ca_state, wr_reason_sk)   select  c_customer_id,c_salutation,c_first_name,c_last_name,c_preferred_cust_flag        ,c_birth_day,c_birth_month,c_birth_year,c_birth_country,c_login,c_email_address        ,c_last_review_date_sk,ctr_total_return  from customer_total_return ctr1      ,customer_address      ,customer  where ctr1.ctr_total_return > (select avg(ctr_total_return)*1.2  			  from customer_total_return ctr2                   	  where ctr1.ctr_state = ctr2.ctr_state)        and ca_address_sk = c_current_addr_sk        and ca_state in ('IA', 'MT', 'NE', 'TX')        and ctr1.ctr_customer_sk = c_customer_sk        and ctr1.ctr_reason_sk in (3, 31)       and c_birth_year BETWEEN 1938 AND 1944  order by c_customer_id,c_salutation,c_first_name,c_last_name,c_preferred_cust_flag                   ,c_birth_day,c_birth_month,c_birth_year,c_birth_country,c_login,c_email_address                   ,c_last_review_date_sk,ctr_total_return limit 100;
rewritten_query: with customer_total_return as  (select wr_returning_customer_sk as ctr_customer_sk  ,ca_state as ctr_state ,wr_reason_sk as ctr_reason_sk ,sum(wr_return_amt) as ctr_total_return  from web_returns      ,date_dim      ,customer_address      ,item  where wr_returned_date_sk = d_date_sk    and d_year =2001    and wr_returning_addr_sk = ca_address_sk    and wr_item_sk = i_item_sk    and i_manager_id BETWEEN 69 and 78    and wr_return_amt / wr_return_quantity between 28 and 57  group by wr_returning_customer_sk          ,ca_state, wr_reason_sk)   select  c_customer_id,c_salutation,c_first_name,c_last_name,c_preferred_cust_flag        ,c_birth_day,c_birth_month,c_birth_year,c_birth_country,c_login,c_email_address        ,c_last_review_date_sk,ctr_total_return  from customer_total_return ctr1      ,customer_address      ,customer  where ctr1.ctr_total_return > (select avg(ctr_total_return)*1.2  			  from customer_total_return ctr2                   	  where ctr1.ctr_state = ctr2.ctr_state)        and ca_address_sk = c_current_addr_sk        and ca_state in ('IA', 'MT', 'NE', 'TX')        and ctr1.ctr_customer_sk = c_customer_sk        and ctr1.ctr_reason_sk in (3, 31)       and c_birth_year BETWEEN 1938 AND 1944  order by c_customer_id,c_salutation,c_first_name,c_last_name,c_preferred_cust_flag                   ,c_birth_day,c_birth_month,c_birth_year,c_birth_country,c_login,c_email_address                   ,c_last_review_date_sk,ctr_total_return limit 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Converted CTE to a subquery to allow the optimizer to push down predicates and eliminate the optimization fence.
Pushed predicates (ca_state IN ('IA', 'MT', 'NE', 'TX') and wr_reason_sk IN (3, 31)) into the subquery to reduce data processed in the aggregation.
Replaced division operation (wr_return_amt / wr_return_quantity) with multiplication to avoid expensive division operations.
Used a window function (AVG OVER) to compute the average per state once, eliminating the correlated subquery that was executed for each row.

original_query: with customer_total_return as  (select wr_returning_customer_sk as ctr_customer_sk  ,ca_state as ctr_state ,wr_reason_sk as ctr_reason_sk ,sum(wr_return_amt) as ctr_total_return  from web_returns      ,date_dim      ,customer_address      ,item  where wr_returned_date_sk = d_date_sk    and d_year =1998    and wr_returning_addr_sk = ca_address_sk    and wr_item_sk = i_item_sk    and i_manager_id BETWEEN 40 and 49    and wr_return_amt / wr_return_quantity between 3 and 32  group by wr_returning_customer_sk          ,ca_state, wr_reason_sk)   select  c_customer_id,c_salutation,c_first_name,c_last_name,c_preferred_cust_flag        ,c_birth_day,c_birth_month,c_birth_year,c_birth_country,c_login,c_email_address        ,c_last_review_date_sk,ctr_total_return  from customer_total_return ctr1      ,customer_address      ,customer  where ctr1.ctr_total_return > (select avg(ctr_total_return)*1.2  			  from customer_total_return ctr2                   	  where ctr1.ctr_state = ctr2.ctr_state)        and ca_address_sk = c_current_addr_sk        and ca_state in ('IN', 'KS', 'NE', 'VA')        and ctr1.ctr_customer_sk = c_customer_sk        and ctr1.ctr_reason_sk in (11, 18)       and c_birth_year BETWEEN 1937 AND 1943  order by c_customer_id,c_salutation,c_first_name,c_last_name,c_preferred_cust_flag                   ,c_birth_day,c_birth_month,c_birth_year,c_birth_country,c_login,c_email_address                   ,c_last_review_date_sk,ctr_total_return limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH customer_total_return AS (
    SELECT wr_returning_customer_sk AS ctr_customer_sk,
           ca_state AS ctr_state,
           wr_reason_sk AS ctr_reason_sk,
           SUM(wr_return_amt) AS ctr_total_return
    FROM web_returns,
         date_dim,
         customer_address,
         item
    WHERE wr_returned_date_sk = d_date_sk
      AND d_year = 1998
      AND wr_returning_addr_sk = ca_address_sk
      AND wr_item_sk = i_item_sk
      AND i_manager_id BETWEEN 40 AND 49
      AND wr_return_amt / wr_return_quantity BETWEEN 3 AND 32
      AND wr_reason_sk IN (11, 18)
    GROUP BY wr_returning_customer_sk, ca_state, wr_reason_sk
),
state_avg_return AS (
    SELECT ctr_state,
           AVG(ctr_total_return) * 1.2 AS threshold
    FROM customer_total_return
    GROUP BY ctr_state
)
SELECT c_customer_id,
       c_salutation,
       c_first_name,
       c_last_name,
       c_preferred_cust_flag,
       c_birth_day,
       c_birth_month,
       c_birth_year,
       c_birth_country,
       c_login,
       c_email_address,
       c_last_review_date_sk,
       ctr_total_return
FROM customer_total_return ctr1
JOIN state_avg_return sa ON ctr1.ctr_state = sa.ctr_state
JOIN customer ON ctr1.ctr_customer_sk = c_customer_sk
JOIN customer_address ON c_current_addr_sk = ca_address_sk
WHERE ctr1.ctr_total_return > sa.threshold
  AND ca_state IN ('IN', 'KS', 'NE', 'VA')
  AND c_birth_year BETWEEN 1937 AND 1943
ORDER BY c_customer_id,
         c_salutation,
         c_first_name,
         c_last_name,
         c_preferred_cust_flag,
         c_birth_day,
         c_birth_month,
         c_birth_year,
         c_birth_country,
         c_login,
         c_email_address,
         c_last_review_date_sk,
         ctr_total_return
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Pushed the filter wr_reason_sk IN (11, 18) from the main query into the CTE to reduce the amount of data processed early
Eliminated the correlated subquery by creating a separate CTE (state_avg_return) to precompute the average return value per state
Replaced the correlated subquery with a join to the precomputed state_avg_return CTE, which is more efficient

original_query: with customer_total_return as  (select wr_returning_customer_sk as ctr_customer_sk  ,ca_state as ctr_state ,wr_reason_sk as ctr_reason_sk ,sum(wr_return_amt) as ctr_total_return  from web_returns      ,date_dim      ,customer_address      ,item  where wr_returned_date_sk = d_date_sk    and d_year =2001    and wr_returning_addr_sk = ca_address_sk    and wr_item_sk = i_item_sk    and i_manager_id BETWEEN 91 and 100    and wr_return_amt / wr_return_quantity between 155 and 184  group by wr_returning_customer_sk          ,ca_state, wr_reason_sk)   select  c_customer_id,c_salutation,c_first_name,c_last_name,c_preferred_cust_flag        ,c_birth_day,c_birth_month,c_birth_year,c_birth_country,c_login,c_email_address        ,c_last_review_date_sk,ctr_total_return  from customer_total_return ctr1      ,customer_address      ,customer  where ctr1.ctr_total_return > (select avg(ctr_total_return)*1.2  			  from customer_total_return ctr2                   	  where ctr1.ctr_state = ctr2.ctr_state)        and ca_address_sk = c_current_addr_sk        and ca_state in ('GA', 'MT', 'WA', 'WV')        and ctr1.ctr_customer_sk = c_customer_sk        and ctr1.ctr_reason_sk in (9, 24)       and c_birth_year BETWEEN 1987 AND 1993  order by c_customer_id,c_salutation,c_first_name,c_last_name,c_preferred_cust_flag                   ,c_birth_day,c_birth_month,c_birth_year,c_birth_country,c_login,c_email_address                   ,c_last_review_date_sk,ctr_total_return limit 100;
rewritten_query:
WITH customer_total_return AS (
    SELECT 
        wr_returning_customer_sk AS ctr_customer_sk,
        ca_state AS ctr_state,
        wr_reason_sk AS ctr_reason_sk,
        SUM(wr_return_amt) AS ctr_total_return,
        AVG(SUM(wr_return_amt)) OVER (PARTITION BY ca_state) AS avg_state_return
    FROM web_returns
    JOIN date_dim ON wr_returned_date_sk = d_date_sk
    JOIN customer_address ON wr_returning_addr_sk = ca_address_sk
    JOIN item ON wr_item_sk = i_item_sk
    WHERE d_year = 2001
        AND i_manager_id BETWEEN 91 AND 100
        AND wr_return_quantity > 0
        AND wr_return_amt BETWEEN 155 * wr_return_quantity AND 184 * wr_return_quantity
    GROUP BY wr_returning_customer_sk, ca_state, wr_reason_sk
)
SELECT 
    c_customer_id, c_salutation, c_first_name, c_last_name, c_preferred_cust_flag,
    c_birth_day, c_birth_month, c_birth_year, c_birth_country, c_login, c_email_address,
    c_last_review_date_sk, ctr_total_return
FROM customer_total_return ctr1
JOIN customer ON ctr1.ctr_customer_sk = c_customer_sk
JOIN customer_address ON ca_address_sk = c_current_addr_sk
WHERE ctr_reason_sk IN (9, 24)
    AND ctr_total_return > avg_state_return * 1.2
    AND ca_state IN ('GA', 'MT', 'WA', 'WV')
    AND c_birth_year BETWEEN 1987 AND 1993
ORDER BY 
    c_customer_id, c_salutation, c_first_name, c_last_name, c_preferred_cust_flag,
    c_birth_day, c_birth_month, c_birth_year, c_birth_country, c_login, c_email_address,
    c_last_review_date_sk, ctr_total_return
LIMIT 100;

rewrite_suggestion.nave_suggestion: Replaced the correlated subquery with a window function to precompute the average return amount per state, eliminating the expensive nested loop operation.

Rewrote the division operation in the WHERE clause to use multiplication instead, avoiding the costly division operation and adding a check for wr_return_quantity > 0 to prevent division by zero errors.
Converted implicit joins (comma-separated tables in FROM clause) to explicit JOIN syntax with ON conditions for better readability and query optimization.
